Title: Ectopic Expression of the Allium cepa 1-SST Gene in Cotton Improves Drought Tolerance and Yield Under Drought Stress in the Field

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Kestose, the specific fructan product of Ac1-SST, was detected only in Ac1-SST transgenic cotton and not in wild-type plants, and its content increased 3.6-fold (field) and 2.5-fold (greenhouse) under drought versus normal irrigation, confirming functional activity of the onion 1-SST enzyme in the cotton background. Sucrose content was significantly lower in transgenic lines than in wild-type under drought, consistent with its conversion to 1-kestose. Transgenic lines carried a single T-DNA insertion and showed stable inheritance of Ac1-SST over at least six generations.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Ac1-SST from onion was cloned and stably integrated as a single-copy transgene into cotton, with confirmed expression in leaves across multiple generations.
2. Ac1-SST-overexpressing cotton plants accumulated 1-kestose (absent in wild-type) and showed higher levels of total soluble sugars, glucose, fructose, and other soluble carbohydrates, particularly under drought stress, accompanied by reduced sucrose levels.
3. Under drought, transgenic plants exhibited higher proline and relative water content and lower MDA than wild-type, indicating improved osmotic adjustment and reduced membrane lipid peroxidation.
4. Transgenic lines maintained higher photosynthetic rates, PSII performance (Fv/Fm, ΦPSII, PI_total), and water use efficiency under drought compared with wild-type.
5. In multi-year field trials under drought, Ac1-SST transgenic lines had more fruiting branches and bolls and significantly higher seed yield per plant and per plot, with reduced yield loss relative to wild-type.
6. These results demonstrate that Ac1-SST functions as an effective fructan biosynthesis gene in cotton, enhancing drought tolerance and yield, and represents a promising gene for breeding drought-resistant cotton and other crops.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of Ac1-SST in cotton enhances drought tolerance and maintains or increases yield under drought by increasing 1-kestose and other soluble sugars, elevating proline and relative water content, reducing malondialdehyde accumulation, and sustaining photosynthetic and PSII performance under water-limiting conditions.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Ac1-SST catalyzes transfer of a fructosyl moiety from one sucrose molecule to another to form 1-kestose and glucose, reducing sucrose and increasing low-DP fructans and other soluble carbohydrates, which enhances osmotic adjustment and water retention, reduces membrane lipid peroxidation (lower MDA), and contributes to maintaining photosynthetic capacity and growth under drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Abiotic stress (drought) response mediated by fructan-driven osmotic adjustment, membrane protection, and maintenance of photosynthetic performance in transgenic cotton.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fructan biosynthesis pathway; sucrose:sucrose 1-fructosyltransferase catalyzing formation of 1-kestose from sucrose (low-degree polymerization fructan).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sucrose:sucrose 1-fructosyltransferase (1-SST), a fructosyltransferase enzyme involved in fructan synthesis.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Ac1-SST is constitutively expressed from the CaMV 35S promoter in cotton leaves, with detectable transcripts in all three transgenic lines under both normal irrigation and drought; transcript levels and Ac1-SST enzyme activity are higher under drought stress than under well-watered conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed by withholding irrigation: in the field, plants received only natural precipitation after germination (soil water content can drop to ~11%) compared with normally irrigated controls; in the greenhouse, watering was stopped for up to 32 days (16 days for some enzyme assays) for 45-day-old plants; well-watered plants served as controls.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Engineering fructan metabolism to improve drought tolerance and yield in cotton by ectopic expression of a key fructan biosynthesis gene (Ac1-SST) from onion.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought tolerance) and yield-related traits (fruiting branch number, boll number, seed yield) in cotton.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In field drought trials, transgenic lines increased seed yield per plant by an average of 28.2% and 20.6% compared with wild-type in 2019 and 2020, and plot seed yield increased by 34.1% and 21.1%, respectively. Yield loss per square meter under drought was reduced by an average of 20.9% over two consecutive years. Under drought, transgenic plants had significantly higher numbers of fruiting branches (up to ~69% increase) and bolls (up to ~79.8% increase) relative to wild-type. Kestose content in leaves of transgenic lines under drought was 3.6-fold (field) and 2.5-fold (greenhouse) higher than under normal irrigation. Proline, total soluble sugars, glucose, fructose, and other soluble sugars were significantly higher, while MDA was significantly lower, in transgenic lines than in wild-type under drought. Photosynthetic parameters (Pn, Fv/Fm, ΦPSII, PI_total, WUE) were significantly higher in transgenic lines than in wild-type under drought.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under well-watered conditions, Ac1-SST transgenic cotton lines showed increased number of fruiting branches, boll number, and seed yield per plant compared with wild-type, with slightly reduced plant height. Under drought, transgenic plants showed greater plant height, more fruiting branches and bolls, and maintained greener, less wilted foliage and more open bolls at maturity than wild-type plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Donor: onion (Allium cepa) leaves as RNA source for Ac1-SST cloning. Host: upland cotton (Gossypium hirsutum) cultivar R15; transgenic lines Ac1-SST9, Ac1-SST26, Ac1-SST35 in T0–T6 generations; grown in greenhouse pots and in field plots at Shihezi, Xinjiang. Vector and bacteria: pBI121-Ac1-SST binary vector with CaMV 35S promoter; Agrobacterium tumefaciens strain GV3101.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: cDNA synthesis from onion leaf RNA and PCR amplification of Ac1-SST; cloning into pGEM-T and subcloning into pBI121 under CaMV 35S promoter; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of cotton hypocotyls; tissue culture and regeneration of transgenic plants; grafting of T0 seedlings; kanamycin resistance screening; genomic PCR and Southern blot for transgene detection and copy number; RT-PCR and qRT-PCR for Ac1-SST expression (with Ubiquitin7 internal control); greenhouse and field drought stress treatments by withholding irrigation; measurement of proline, MDA, relative water content, and soluble carbohydrates (total sugar, sucrose, glucose, fructose, 1-kestose, other soluble sugars) using anthrone-based assays, enzymatic assays, and HPLC; Ac1-SST enzyme activity assay based on kestose production; field measurement of agronomic traits (plant height, fruiting branches, boll number, yield); gas-exchange (Pn, E) and chlorophyll fluorescence (Fv/Fm, ΦPSII, PI_total) measurements in the field; statistical analysis by one-way ANOVA and LSD tests.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of Ac1-SST driven by CaMV 35S promoter in cotton (Gossypium hirsutum R15) via Agrobacterium-mediated transformation; confirmation of transgene presence and copy number by genomic PCR and Southern blot; verification of transcript accumulation by RT-PCR and qRT-PCR; measurement of Ac1-SST enzyme activity in leaves; quantification of 1-kestose and other soluble sugars by HPLC and colorimetric assays; physiological assays for proline, MDA, and relative water content; greenhouse and multi-year field drought trials with agronomic trait and photosynthetic/fluorescence measurements.
Plant_Genes.Field_Trials: Multi-year field experiments at Shihezi, Xinjiang, China (temperate continental arid climate) in 2019–2020 using randomized block design with three replicates; T5–T6 Ac1-SST transgenic lines and wild-type R15 grown under two treatments: normal irrigation versus drought (no irrigation after germination, only natural precipitation); plots of 13.5 m² with about 300 plants per plot; agronomic traits and yield assessed at boll-bearing and harvest stages; photosynthetic and fluorescence parameters measured in the field in 2020.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Ac1-SST was amplified from onion leaf cDNA using primers designed from the full Ac1-SST sequence in GenBank, cloned into pGEM-T Easy for sequencing, and then subcloned into the binary vector pBI121 under the CaMV 35S promoter for Agrobacterium-mediated transformation into cotton.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Ectopic expression of Ac1-SST in cotton significantly improves drought tolerance and maintains seed yield under field drought, reducing yield loss per square meter by about 20.9% over two years and increasing seed yield per plant by ~28.2% and 20.6% in 2019 and 2020. The gene confers higher boll and fruiting branch numbers and better photosynthetic performance under water deficit, indicating that Ac1-SST is a promising candidate gene for breeding drought-tolerant, high-yield cotton and potentially other crops grown in arid environments.
